mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs liquidity support, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 218.0 confirms disinflationary pressure, allowing equity valuations to expand without central bank intervention, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings below 218.0 are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no negative predictive power for 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than negative 0.01 across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion, which boosts equity earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields an insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a low-rate environment signals improving capacity utilization without inflationary risk, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during low-rate periods fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 55 percent.
